name: Juggler
flavor: The circus juggler as duelist — a constant cascade of knives, weighted orbs, and clubs, every one a weapon he can loose or keep. He fights in bursts: hurl a storm of thrown steel and stand exposed, or hold a few back to knock the answer aside.

proficiencies.skills: Choose two from Acrobatics, Performance, Sleight of Hand, Insight, and Deception

parryReskin: You don't dodge — you deflect, knocking an incoming blow aside with an implement still in your hand. The more of your set you have already hurled, the less you have left to guard with; empty-handed, all you can do is wait to catch them back at the start of your next turn.
riposte: On a Full Dodge (your Parry d20 comes up strictly above your effective Parry DC), you catch the blow on a held implement and flick it back in the same motion: the triggering attacker takes [[1d4]] damage of that implement's type, no attack roll and no cost beyond the Parry. You keep the implement — this is a deflection, not a throw.

engine.name: Juggle Set
engine.description: You are always juggling a set of light implements — throwing knives, weighted orbs, clubs. They are your ammunition and your guard at once. You never buy, gather, or track them: at the start of each of your turns you catch your whole set back to full. On your action you decide how many to throw; whatever you keep in hand is what you can Deflect with. This baseline is shared by every Juggler from 1st level; your subclass (chosen at 3rd level) shapes what your implements do.
engine.generation: At the start of each of your turns your set refills to full — you catch every implement back into the cascade, thrown or not. There is no other way to gain or lose implements; they are never spent for good.
engine.spend: On your Attack action you throw any number of implements, from 0 up to your Set Size, as your Barrage. Each implement thrown leaves your hand until your set refills, and every one you have thrown worsens your Deflect (with none in hand you cannot Parry at all).
engine.capFormula: proficiency bonus + 1 (+2 more from Grand Cascade at L5)

features.name: Juggle Set
features.description: You gain the Juggle Set — the ring of implements you always keep in the air. See your engine for exactly how it refills, how many you hold (your Set Size), and how throwing them feeds your Barrage and drains your Deflect. Your Set Size is 3 at 1st level and grows as you gain levels.

features.name: Barrage
features.description: When you take the Attack action, you loose your Barrage instead of making a normal attack: choose any number of implements from 0 up to your Set Size and throw them at targets you can see within normal thrown range (20 feet, or up to 60 at disadvantage). Each thrown implement is a separate ranged {{attack roll|d20 + Dexterity modifier + proficiency bonus}}. On a hit, the FIRST of your implements to land each turn deals [[1d4+Dex]]; every implement that hits after that deals only its [[1d4]] die, no ability modifier — a barrage is a flurry, not a dozen full-power strikes. Any Weapon Mastery your implements carry (such as Topple or Slow) triggers at most once per Barrage, however many implements hit. You may split them across different targets or pile them all onto one. Throwing 0 is a real choice — you make no attack but keep your full set in hand for defense (see Deflect).

features.name: Flourish
features.description: The more of your set you commit to a Barrage, the flashier the trick — but you get only ONE flourish per turn, decided by how many implements you throw. You gain the single benefit for your throw count, not the lower ones (throw 2 and you get the second effect only, not the first):

options.name: Throw 1
options.effect: Pinpoint — you have advantage on that attack roll. A single aimed throw made with your guard still up (you keep almost your whole set for Deflect).

options.name: Throw 2
options.effect: Off-Balance — a creature you hit with an implement this turn has disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

options.name: Throw 3 or more
options.effect: Showstopper — for the rest of this turn your thrown implements score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20, the all-in flurry finding every gap. Committing three or more implements badly weakens your Deflect this round (and empties your hands entirely at low levels), so the crit-fishing costs you your guard.

features.name: Deflect
features.description: Your Parry is a deflection made with an implement still in hand, so how well you can do it depends on how many you are still holding. Your effective Parry DC is your base of 12 while you hold your full set, and it worsens by 1 for each implement you have thrown and not yet caught back — that is, +1 for each one short of a full set. If you are holding no implements at all, you cannot Parry until your set refills at the start of your next turn. Because the set refills every turn, this resets each round: a turn you throw everything is a turn you stand defenseless, and a turn you hold some back keeps your guard up at a worse DC. Deflecting still costs your reaction like any Parry.

features.name: Nimble Cascade
features.description: A juggler never plants his feet. As a bonus action on your turn, you can move up to half your speed, and that movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks. Open the range before you empty your set, or slip away on a turn you're left defenseless.

features.name: Ricochet
features.description: Your implements carom off their targets. Once on each of your turns, when a thrown implement hits a creature during your Barrage, you can have it ricochet outward from that target: make a separate ranged attack roll against every other creature you can see within 15 feet of it, and on each hit the implement deals [[1d4+Dex]] damage (rolled separately for each). Only ONE implement ricochets per turn, no matter how many you throw — so a full 3-implement Barrage still gets a single ricochet. The ricochet is a free bonus of an implement you already threw: it doesn't cost you an implement or further worsen your Deflect.

features.name: Trick Shot
features.description: You don't just throw hard — you throw smart, reading angles and gaps mid-cascade. Once on each of your turns you can designate ONE implement in your Barrage as a Trick Shot — never more than that single implement, no matter how many you throw. On a hit, in addition to its normal damage, the target must succeed on a saving throw against your {{Trick Shot DC|6 + proficiency bonus + Intelligence modifier}} or suffer one effect of your choice (the save ability depends on the effect):

options.name: Blind
options.effect: An orb bursts across its eyes — Constitution save or blinded until the end of its next turn.

options.name: Trip
options.effect: A club takes its legs — Strength save or knocked prone.

options.name: Disarm
options.effect: A knife to the knuckles — Strength save or it drops one item it is holding (your choice), landing at its feet.

options.name: Pin
options.effect: The implement staples a limb in place — Dexterity save or its speed becomes 0 until the end of its next turn.

features.name: Grand Cascade
features.description: Your cascade widens. Your Set Size increases by 2 — it is now your proficiency bonus + 3 — so you juggle, hold, and can throw that many more implements each turn. Every extra one you keep still shores up your Deflect, and every extra one you throw still feeds your Barrage, Ricochet, and Trick Shot.

features.name: Chain Reaction
features.description: Keep the show going. Once per combat, you can choose to make your Ricochet chain: after it sprays its targets, pick one creature it hit and the implement caroms off and ricochets a second time from that creature's space — a separate ranged attack against every other creature you can see within 15 feet of it, dealing [[1d4+Dex]] on each hit, exactly like Ricochet. You decide when to trigger it; it does not require dropping a target.

keyStats.label: Set Size
keyStats.formula: proficiency bonus + 1 (Grand Cascade adds +2 more from L5)
keyStats.note: How many implements you hold and can throw each turn.

keyStats.label: Implement Damage
keyStats.note: First implement to hit each turn adds Dex; the rest deal the die only. Die scales at L5/11/17.

keyStats.label: Trick Shot DC
keyStats.note: Save DC for Trick Shot effects and your subclass's forced saves (from L3). Modest in practice since Int is a secondary stat.

keyStats.label: Parry Base DC
keyStats.note: At full hands; +1 per implement thrown, none held = no Parry. Lower is better.
